title: A United Flight Turned Around Mid-Atlantic Over a Bluetooth Name
script: A United Airlines flight bound for Spain turned around mid-Atlantic and flew back to New Jersey. The reason: a passenger's phone Bluetooth network was named something that scared the crew. The plane had already been airborne for hours when the captain made the call. 264 passengers got grounded. United said the threat wasn't real, just a name. The FAA is now looking at whether new rules are needed. One Wi-Fi nickname cost an airline tens of thousands of dollars.
